rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MILTON COHN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #11, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/17/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. Although file stamped on 11/5/24, the amended petition was not e-filed until 11/6/24, when it was first available to be viewed on the Court's website. [LRSF 14.14 A.] (2) It appears that bond could have been reduced in prior years or should now be reduced. The petition does not disclose how much the bond is, but the successor trustee filed proof of $976,000 bond in 2013. (3) Who is the proposed successor trustee, Monica Levavi. Has she consented to serve? The current successor trustee is resigning...? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/3/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LOVIE MAE PHILLIPS
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the Court to consider whether to take this petition off calendar, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 09/20/2024 - see also tentative ruling for 12/02/2024 hearing) remain unaddressed: 1) Victor Phillips' zip code listed on the proof of service appears incorrect. 2) Each status report must include all allegations required by LRSF 14.35(J)(1), even if they were made in the previous report. 3) The decedent died more than 2 years ago, but no first interim account has been filed. Does the petitioner anticipate collecting waivers of account from both beneficiaries? See LRSF 14.34(A) & Probate Code section 10950. If the beneficiaries do not waive the petitioner's account, the Court is to determine whether to order a first interim account covering a 2-year period from the date of death, and if so, whether a new petition consisting of the first account and updated status report should be required, as the current petition (status report) cannot be amended to add the first account (higher filing fee and different disposition code will apply to accounting petitions). 4) The petition only requested a continuance of administration until 12/01/2024. The petition must be amended and renoticed if a petition for final distribution is not ready to be filed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANNA Q. WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/24/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The only correct form of notice in a decedent's estate is the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120 (except for an initial petition for appointment of a personal representative or an 850 petition). The proof of service on the reverse side of that form should be completed. If a pleading form of proof of service is filed, a copy of the Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing that was served must be attached to it. (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) The administrator was required by law to file his Final I&A within four months from 12/5/23. [Probate Code Sections 8800(b) and (c).] Since the administrator requests additional time to administer the estate through 2/25/25, it is expected that his petition for final distribution will be filed well before the continued hearing for this report and petition, in which case this report of status will be taken off calendar. If he has not filed the Final I&A (and his petition for final distribution) by 2/25/25, an appearance will be required on 3/24/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE HARLAN SEE TRUST, DATED SEPTEMBER 11, 1990, AS RESTATED APRIL 14, 2010 AND AS AMENDED APRIL 11,
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For: 1. Order Compelling Trustee To Perform Duties Pursuant To Trust; 2. Breach Of Fiduciary Duties By Initial Successor Trustee; 3. Suspension Of Initial Successor Trustee Pending Removal; 4. Removal Of Initial Successor Trustee; 5. Attorney'S Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/26/2025 (no earlier date available), as the following issues (see tentative rulings for 09/25/2024 & 11/20/2024 hearings) remain unaddressed: 1) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form on each person entitled to notice at least 30 days before the hearing. The DE-120 form filed on 10/30/2024 shows the date of mailing as 10/28/2024, less than 30 days before the 11/20/2024 hearing. New notice must be given. 2) Melinda (or Malinda) Randolph's consent to act as successor trustee is not on file. Please confirm the first name, as the operative trust documents refer to "Malinda", while the petition refers to "Melinda". 3) The proposed order includes a finding that the respondent is deemed to have predeceased the settlor, but the petition and prayer for relief did not. In addition, a review of the first amendment filed on 12/05/2024 indicates that the following issues must be addressed: A) New notice of the amendment must be given at least 30 days before the hearing. See CRC 7.53(a). B) If Beverly Johnson survived the settlor for 60 days, notice must be given to her personal representative. Please provide the name and address of the personal representative, together with a copy of letters. If there is no personal representative, please provide the name and address of each heir and devisee of Beverly Johnson and each executor named in her will, and notice must be given to each person. See Probate Code section 17203(c).
